summary: Legendary New Speedway Boogie breakout & scorching Althea jam
blurb: The set opens with a punchy Bertha and erupts into the surprise New Speedway Boogie breakout. The second set delivers deep jams—China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider > Crazy Fingers—and a fiery Althea performance. Jerry’s solos, especially on Black Peter, are standout moments.
review: February 19, 1991 at the Oakland‑Alameda County Coliseum captures a Grateful Dead that was both tight and adventurous. The first set builds momentum with a bright Bertha before surprising the crowd with a rare New Speedway Boogie breakout that has Jerry ripping through the groove and delivering a whaling solo on Black Peter later in the night. The second set is where the magic truly unfolds: China Cat Sunflower flows seamlessly into I Know You Rider, then launches into an expansive Crazy Fingers jam that keeps the band locked in for the remainder of the set. A scorching Althea jam showcases Jerry “on fire,” while the rhythm section drives forward with unrelenting energy. Though some mixes tilt toward a loud Bob guitar and overly bright Vince keyboards—especially on the soundboard sources—the performance itself remains solid, making this show a must‑listen for fans who love late‑period improvisation paired with classic songcraft.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Strong positive sentiment across multiple recordings: 'legendary New Speedway Boogie breakout', 'scorching Althea jam', and 'deep jams' are repeatedly praised, while no consistent negative comments about musical performance appear; only mix issues are noted.

best_recording.reason: Clear soundboard capture that includes the full set with good overall fidelity; despite occasional mix imbalances, it offers the most complete and listenable version of the performance.

key_highlights: New Speedway Boogie breakout energizes the first set
Althea jam showcases Jerry on fire with a scorching improvisation
China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider > Crazy Fingers sequence delivers deep second‑set jams
Black Peter features a whaling solo from Jerry and a soulful mood

band_performance.Jerry: Ripping on New Speedway Boogie, delivering a whaling solo on Black Peter and staying "on fire" during the Althea jam; occasional mix issues mask some leads but his playing remains stellar throughout.
band_performance.Bob: Rhythm guitar is notably loud in the mix—sometimes comically so—but adds powerful energy to the set.
band_performance.Brent: Vince's keyboards sit high in the mix, occasionally stepping on Jerry, yet they help define the modern 1990s sound of the band.
